description: IBM Equal Access rule IDs owned by shared app chrome (header, folder sidebar) or third-party widgets (Radix, AG Grid, cmdk) that individual feature markup cannot fix. Single source of truth: a11y specs import this to build their runA11yScan ignoreRules, and build-a11y-html-report.mjs reads it to mark these findings as suppressed (greyed, not dropped) so real feature-owned issues stand out. Removing a rule here re-surfaces it everywhere.

suppressed.reason: Left folder sidebar (MainPage chrome) and Radix portal menus rendered on document.body — shared chrome/framework portals, not feature markup.
Header GitHub button (aria-label "GitHub 151k" vs visible "151k") — shared header chrome.
Global theme tokens (text-muted-foreground ~4.40:1, text-accent-emerald-foreground ~3.86:1) used app-wide. Real, tracked Level-1 gap to resolve at the theme level, not per feature.
AG-Grid tab-guard divs (tabindex=0 role=presentation) and Radix focus guards — framework-owned, not fixable from application code.
AG-Grid exposes no gridOptions/GridApi to set an aria-label on its role=treegrid root.
AG-Grid rowgroups and cmdk's role=listbox drive selection via aria-activedescendant (the correct composite-widget pattern), so they have no individually tabbable descendants.
Radix sets aria-hidden on the entire background subtree without `inert` when an overlay opens, so focusable descendants behind it stay tabbable. Flags app-wide chrome, not per-element defects.
cmdk's <Command> root always renders an empty visually-hidden <label>; with no label prop it has no content — framework-owned.
cmdk's hidden label htmlFor targets the CommandInput that only mounts while the popup is open — framework-owned.
cmdk's CommandInput hardcodes aria-autocomplete=list role=combobox on the input inside the popup, which the checker treats as misplaced relative to the trigger — framework-owned.
